user_query: I maintain a small internal knowledge site on biotechnology patent examination and I need an on-page SEO audit and focused optimizations based on our local metrics. Inputs: - HTML pages: input/site/*.html (4 files provided) - Page targets and metrics: input/targets.csv (maps filename to primary keyword, secondary keywords, impressions, ctr) Please do the following and produce all deliverables under the output/ directory: 1) Audit all HTML files in input/site against these deterministic rules: - Title length target: 50–60 characters. Meta description length target: 120–160 characters. - Primary keyword must appear (case-insensitive substring match) in both the and the <meta name="description"> content to be considered present. - h1_present is true if at least one <h1> exists. - keyword_in_h1 is true if any <h1> contains the primary keyword (case-insensitive substring). - keyword_in_body is true if the primary keyword appears at least once in visible <p> text (case-insensitive substring). - For each page, compute issue_weight as: • start at 1.0 • if <title> missing, +1.0; else if title length <50 or >60, +0.5 • if meta description missing, +1.0; else if meta length <120 or >160, +0.5 • if no <h1>, +0.5 • if the primary keyword is not present in the title, +0.5 - Compute priority_score = impressions * (1 - ctr) * issue_weight (ctr is the decimal in the CSV). 2) Generate two CSVs: - output/seo_audit.csv with columns (in this order): filename, primary_keyword, title, title_length, meta_description, meta_length, h1_present, keyword_in_title, keyword_in_meta, keyword_in_h1, keyword_in_body, impressions, ctr, issue_weight, priority_score. - output/priority_ranked.csv containing the same filename, primary_keyword, impressions, ctr, issue_weight, priority_score, and a rank column (1 = highest priority). Sort by priority_score descending; break ties by higher impressions, then by filename A–Z. 3) Edit only the top 3 pages by rank and write updated copies to output/site/<same filename>: - Rewrite the <title> to include the primary keyword and be between 50 and 60 characters inclusive. - Add or rewrite the <meta name="description"> to include the primary keyword and be between 120 and 160 characters inclusive. - Do not change any other content (e.g., do not add/remove <h1> or alter body text). 4) Produce a change log and a short executive summary: - output/changes.csv with columns: filename, old_title, new_title, old_meta_description, new_meta_description (leave fields empty if previously missing). - output/summary.md that briefly explains the methodology, lists the top 3 pages with their priority_score and main issues addressed, and summarizes how many pages were audited and how many were updated. Assumptions and constraints: - Match pages by the filename field in input/targets.csv. - Treat all length counts as the number of characters in the text content of the tag or attribute. - Case-insensitive substring checks for keyword presence. - Work entirely offline using the provided files; do not fetch external content. Deliverables must be reproducible from the inputs. If anything is unclear, make a reasonable assumption and document it in output/summary.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
